fields.description:
RED baseline test suite: inject the full LLM-failure matrix through the pydantic-ai `model_override` seam and pin rebar's CURRENT behavior, so later stories flip it to the new behavior with a regression net.

## Why
Before changing behavior we must pin what rebar does today for every failure class and prove the offline injection harness works. The brainstorm validated on pydantic-ai 1.107.0 that a `FunctionModel` which raises `ModelHTTPError(429)` propagates with `status_code` intact, a canned `finish_reason` is readable at `result.response.finish_reason`, and `httpx.ReadTimeout` propagates unwrapped â€” so the harness is viable with no live/billable call.

## What
A parametrized RED test file injecting each failure class via a raising or canned pydantic-ai `FunctionModel` through the `PydanticAIRunner(model_override=...)` / `get_runner(override=...)` seams (`src/rebar/llm/runner.py:164,400`), asserting current behavior at two levels: (1) runner classification (everything collapses to `LLMUnavailableError` / `LLMRunnerError` today) and (2) gate end-to-end (plan-review systemic â†’ INDETERMINATE + claim blocked; completion â†’ fail-closed; code-review). Guarded by `pydantic_ai.models.ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS = False`.

## Scope
- Failure matrix: 429-rate, 429-insufficient_quota (body), 529, 500/503, `httpx.ConnectTimeout`, `httpx.ReadTimeout`, 401/403, 400 bad-request, 400 context-length, 413, `content_filter` finish_reason, `ContentFilterError`, length/truncation, `IncompleteToolCall`, unparseable output, validation-retry exhaustion, `UsageLimitExceeded` (request_limit=50), `tool_calls_limit`, unknown exception.
- Two-scope scenarios: systemic (whole op) vs per-criterion (one unit among N).
- Named liveness scenarios pin CURRENT (pre-change) behavior only: a hung model request and a hung tool document that TODAY there is no per-request/per-tool timeout (the RED test asserts the current unbounded/hanging behavior, e.g. via a short outer test-level guard), and a looping model reaches the default `request_limit=50`. The NEW abort+classify assertions (the `run_sync`-compatible zombie-run defense) are added by story fd1d's tests, NOT here â€” this baseline story must stay green on today's code.
- Guard tests: `--force` / `--force-close` / feature-disabled never enters the handling path.
- Coverage boundary: `model_override` injects at the pydantic-ai MODEL layer, which is ABOVE the httpx transport where the retry lives â€” so transport-layer retry is NOT exercisable here; it is covered by the transport-injection spike in story 95a5. This suite covers classification + gate behavior + silent-success, not the transport.
- NOT in scope: the NEW behavior (asserted GREEN by later stories) â€” this story pins CURRENT behavior only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A parametrized test file (e.g. `tests/unit/test_llm_failure_matrix.py`) exercises every failure class via `model_override`, offline, guarded by `ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS=False`.
- [ ] Each case asserts the current runner-level and gate-level behavior with a source citation comment.
- [ ] Two-scope (systemic vs per-criterion) and force/disabled guard scenarios are covered.
- [ ] `make test` passes; the file is the named baseline that later stories flip to the new behavior.
